description: Mechanical rubric for Silo C (Behavioral spec — what the Phishield scanner does TODAY). Each case is verifiable by grep / JSON path lookup / count / schema validation against substrate, source code, or fixture simulator output. No human judgement required.

cases.description: Spec enumerates a checker count and reconciles NUM-001 (22 vs 26 vs 27)
cases.expected: total_count == 27 distinct checker classes; reconciliation explicitly addresses NUM-001 stating: '22' = stale memory, '26' = manual scoring categories, '27' = code reality
cases.evaluator_logic: Load spec; assert spec.checker_inventory.total_count == 27 AND spec.checker_inventory.reconciliation contains tokens {'22','26','27','NUM-001'} AND a one-line rationale per number.

cases.description: Spec lists every checker class with file:line locator that exists in source
cases.expected: For each entry, grep `class <ClassName>` in the cited file at within +/- 5 lines of line_start returns exactly one match
cases.evaluator_logic: For each checker entry, run Grep pattern `^class <ClassName>` in spec.file; require >= 1 hit AND |hit_line - spec.line_start| <= 5.

cases.description: Spec includes the 4 classes whose file:line locators are load-bearing
cases.expected: Set must include {'EmailSecurityChecker','SSLChecker','ShodanVulnChecker','GlasswingPartnerChecker'} all with non-null file:line
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert each of the 4 names appears in spec.checker_inventory.checkers AND each has file ending '.py' AND line_start is int > 0.

cases.description: Spec assigns each checker to its execution phase
cases.expected: phase in {'phase2_lightweight','phase2_heavy','phase2_conditional','phase3_per_ip','enrichment'}; SSLChecker and SubdomainChecker are phase2_heavy; ShodanVulnChecker, DNSInfrastructureChecker, HighRiskProtocolChecker, DNSBLChecker are phase3_per_ip; FraudulentDomainChecker is phase2_conditional
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert phase enum membership for every entry; assert specific assignments listed above match.

cases.description: Spec marks checkers that require external API keys with their env var name
cases.expected: BreachChecker->HIBP_API_KEY, DehashedChecker->DEHASHED_API_KEY, VirusTotalChecker->VIRUSTOTAL_API_KEY, SecurityTrailsChecker->SECURITYTRAILS_API_KEY, ShodanVulnChecker->SHODAN_API_KEY, IntelXChecker->INTELX_API_KEY
cases.evaluator_logic: For each pair above assert spec entry has matching api_key_env; for non-API checkers assert api_key_env is null/absent.

cases.description: Spec reproduces the WEIGHTS dict exactly
cases.expected: Dict equal to {ssl:0.09, exposed_admin:0.09, high_risk_protocols:0.08, breaches:0.07, shodan_vulns:0.07, ransomware_risk:0.06, dnsbl:0.06, email_security:0.06, http_headers:0.05, virustotal:0.05, info_disclosure:0.05, tech_stack:0.05, website_security:0.04, vpn_remote:0.04, fraudulent_domains:0.04, dehashed:0.03, external_ips:0.03, data_breach_index:0.03, email_hardening:0.02, subdomains:0.02, payment_security:0.02, privacy_compliance:0.02, web_ranking:0.02, financial_impact:0.02, securitytrails:0.01}
cases.evaluator_logic: Diff spec dict vs canonical dict; require zero key/value differences (float tolerance 1e-6).

cases.description: Spec asserts WEIGHTS sum to ~1.0 (INV-08)
cases.evaluator_logic: Compute sum of spec.scoring.per_checker_weights values; assert abs(sum - 1.0) <= 0.01.

cases.description: Spec states risk-score scale 0-1000 with the 4 inverted bands (INV-03)
cases.expected: scale = '0-1000 lower-is-worse'; bands = {Critical:[0,250], High:[250,500], Medium:[500,750], Low:[750,1000]}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert spec.scoring.scale matches; assert each of the 4 band entries matches bounds exactly.

cases.description: Spec describes inverted-direction convention: per-checker 0-100 higher=better; overall 0-1000 lower=better
cases.expected: Text must contain both 'per-checker 0-100 higher = better' and 'overall 0-1000 lower = better' (or semantic equivalent with both numeric scales)
cases.evaluator_logic: Regex match both phrases (case-insensitive); both must hit.

cases.description: Spec describes failure exclusion + weight redistribution (INV-02)
cases.expected: States: failed statuses = {error, timeout}; skipped = {no_api_key, auth_failed, disabled, skipped}; both excluded; remaining weights renormalised. References scoring_analytics.py:549-576.
cases.evaluator_logic: Grep `_FAILED_STATUSES = {"error", "timeout"}` in scoring_analytics.py — must hit. Spec must enumerate same two sets and cite line range overlapping 549-576.

cases.description: Spec lists exactly 4 compliance frameworks (INV-11)
cases.expected: len == 4 and set == {'POPIA','PCI DSS v4.0','ISO 27001','NIST CSF 2.0'}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert len(spec.compliance.frameworks) == 4 AND name-set equality (case-insensitive trim).

cases.description: Spec asserts each compliance control maps to 1-3 checkers with weight 0.8-1.2
cases.expected: For every control, 1 <= len(checker_mappings) <= 3 AND every mapping.weight in [0.8, 1.2]
cases.evaluator_logic: Iterate all controls; fail if any violates count or weight range.

cases.description: Spec gives the verdict thresholds: Pass all>=80, Fail any<=20, else Partial
cases.expected: Pass: all mapped checker scores >= 80; Fail: any mapped checker score <= 20; Partial: otherwise
cases.evaluator_logic: Three regex/key checks — Pass>=80, Fail<=20, Partial fallback present.

cases.description: Spec cites scoring_analytics.py line range for compliance map
cases.expected: Cites scoring_analytics.py and line range overlapping [228, 450]
cases.evaluator_logic: Parse 'scoring_analytics.py:<a>-<b>'; assert overlap with [228,450].

cases.description: Spec enumerates TEF per industry exactly (INV-10)
cases.expected: {Financial Services:1.45, Healthcare:1.40, Manufacturing:1.15, Retail:1.25, Agriculture:0.80, Other:1.0}
cases.evaluator_logic: Dict equality with float tolerance 1e-6.

cases.description: Spec gives p_breach formula
cases.expected: p_breach = min(1.0, vulnerability * TEF * 0.3) where vulnerability = (100 - overall_score/10) / 100
cases.evaluator_logic: Regex match canonical formula tokens {'min(1.0','vulnerability','TEF','0.3','100 - overall_score/10'}.

cases.description: Spec encodes regulatory exposure formulas (INV-12)
cases.expected: POPIA: min(R10M, rev * 0.02); GDPR (if eu_data): rev * 0.04; PCI (if cards): R1M * (1 - adj_compliance) capped 30% external visibility; Other jurisdictions: R2M each
cases.evaluator_logic: All four formulas must be present with tokens {min(R10M,rev),0.02,0.04,R1M,1 - adj_compliance,30%,R2M}.

cases.description: Spec describes 3-category aggregation post 2026-04-13 restructure (INV-13)
cases.expected: States 7 incident types + 5 cost components -> 3 reporting categories {Breach, Ransomware, BI}; reference profile split Breach 49% / Ransomware 27% / BI 24%
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert presence of '7 incident types', '5 cost components', '3 categories', and split percentages within +/-1 of 49/27/24.

cases.description: Spec describes RSI base 0.05 with [0,1] bound and Glasswing -0.05 floor 0 (INV-09, INV-06)
cases.expected: {base: 0.05, range: [0,1], glasswing_credit: -0.05, glasswing_floor: 0}
cases.evaluator_logic: Numeric equality on all 4 fields.

cases.description: Spec enumerates exactly 15 scans.db tables with names
cases.expected: len == 15; name set == {scans, clients, contacts, quotes, policies, invoices, invoice_line_items, payments, activities, client_notes, records_of_advice, claims, communications, tasks, complaints}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert len == 15 AND set equality on names.

cases.description: Spec records ALTER-TABLE-style migration columns (INV-15)
cases.expected: Must list at least: scans.client_id (added via app.py:149); clients.archived (added via app.py:303); policies.commission_rate, policies.commission_amount (app.py:312-314); complaints.resolution (app.py:908)
cases.evaluator_logic: Grep 'ALTER TABLE' in app.py; cross-reference each spec entry with a real ALTER TABLE statement in cited line.

cases.description: Spec acknowledges no migration framework (INV-15) and single-DB (INV-14)
cases.expected: Explicitly references INV-14 and INV-15 with status 'as-is' (not violated)
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert both INV-14 and INV-15 keys present with status == 'as-is'.

cases.description: Spec enumerates pipeline stages exactly (INV-18)
cases.expected: ['lead','scanned','quoted','bound','renewal'] in this exact order
cases.evaluator_logic: List equality (ordered).

cases.description: Spec enumerates 38 CRM routes
cases.expected: len == 38; each entry has {path, method, handler, file_line}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert len == 38; assert each entry has all 4 keys non-null.

cases.description: Spec describes auto-lead-creation hook on POST /api/scan (INV-19)
cases.expected: States: on POST /api/scan, if no client exists for domain, create with pipeline_stage='lead' and link scan; cites app.py:697-719
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert tokens {'/api/scan','no client','pipeline_stage','lead','app.py:697'}; grep app.py for `pipeline_stage='lead'` near line 697 must hit.

cases.description: Spec acknowledges no auth/RBAC (INV-16) and reseller column dead (INV-17)
cases.expected: INV-16 and INV-17 both present with status 'as-is'
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert both keys present with status == 'as-is'.

cases.description: Spec defines /api/scan request/response shape
cases.expected: request: {domain, industry, annual_revenue, annual_revenue_zar, country, include_fraudulent_domains, client_ips}; response: {scan_id}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert request.required keys superset of {domain,industry,annual_revenue}; assert response.required == ['scan_id'].

cases.description: Spec describes SSE progress stream and PDF/history endpoints (INV-22)
cases.expected: Has GET /api/scan/<id>/progress with SSE event shape {checker, status, score, ips}; GET /api/scan/<id>/pdf?type=full|summary (only these two enums); GET /api/history/<domain> returning last 10 scans
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert all 3 endpoints; assert pdf type enum exactly ['full','summary']; assert SSE event has {checker,status} required.

cases.description: Spec defines /api/scan/<id> top-level response keys
cases.expected: Set superset of {domain_scanned, scan_timestamp, overall_risk_score, risk_level, discovered_ips, scan_context, categories, recommendations, compliance, insurance}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert each of the 10 keys present in spec.output_api.scan_response_schema.required_keys.

cases.description: Spec describes insurance subtree with rsi, financial_impact, dbi, remediation
cases.expected: Keys: rsi (with rsi_score, risk_label, contributing_factors), financial_impact (score, total {most_likely,p5,p50,p95}, scenarios_4cat, insurance_recommendation), dbi (dbi_score, label), remediation (findings)
cases.evaluator_logic: Schema-walk: verify each nested key path present.

cases.description: Spec predicts phishield_fs.json overall_risk_score band and structure
cases.expected: {overall_risk_score: 250-500 (High band per INV-03 OR Medium band 500-750 — pick one with rationale), risk_level matches band, n_discovered_ips: 15-19, has 'insurance.rsi.rsi_score', has 'compliance.POPIA'}. Actual: 294 / Medium / 17 IPs
cases.evaluator_logic: Load fixture; assert spec.predictions.phishield_fs.overall_risk_score band contains 294; assert spec.risk_level == 'Medium'; assert |spec.n_discovered_ips - 17| <= 3; assert spec asserts presence of insurance.rsi.rsi_score and compliance.POPIA paths in fixture.

cases.description: Spec predicts takealot_retail.json overall_risk_score band
cases.expected: overall_risk_score in 'Critical' band (0-250) OR low end of Medium; risk_level == 'Medium'; presence of insurance.rsi.rsi_score and compliance.POPIA. Actual: 235 / Medium
cases.evaluator_logic: Load fixture; assert 235 falls inside spec's predicted band; assert spec.risk_level == 'Medium'; assert presence flags both true.

cases.description: Spec predicts broken_hygiene_synth.json scores in Critical band
cases.expected: overall_risk_score in [0,250] (Critical band); risk_level == 'Critical'; rsi_score >= 0.75. Actual: 125 / Critical / 0.92
cases.evaluator_logic: Load fixture; assert 125 in spec band; spec.risk_level == 'Critical'; assert spec.rsi_score lower bound <= 0.92 <= upper bound.

cases.description: Spec explicitly addresses every invariant INV-01 through INV-31
cases.expected: Dict with keys INV-01..INV-31 (31 entries). Each has {status: 'as-is' | 'deliberately_violated', rationale: <str if violated>}
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert all 31 keys present; for each entry assert status in {'as-is','deliberately_violated'}; if violated, rationale length >= 20 chars.

cases.description: Spec addresses 'no scheduler / pull-only' (INV-23, INV-24, ARCH-001)
cases.expected: Both 'as-is'. Spec must additionally cite ARCH-001 (no scheduler) and ARCH-003 (no incremental/delta) as observed today.
cases.evaluator_logic: Assert both invariants 'as-is'; assert spec.observations contains references to 'ARCH-001' and 'ARCH-003'.
